connectorUiConfig.title: Commvault Security IQ (Serverless)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
The Commvault Security IQ connector enables ingestion of security events and anomalies from Commvault into Microsoft Sentinel. This serverless connector uses the Commvault REST API to automatically collect security events, enabling automated incident creation and response through Analytic Rules and Playbooks.

**Note**: This is the serverless (CCF) version of the Commvault Security IQ connector, which replaces the Azure Function-based connector with native Microsoft Sentinel data collection capabilities.

sampleQueries.query:
{{graphQueriesTableName}}
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Count=count() by EventCode
| order by Count desc

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions are required.

customs.name: Commvault API Access
customs.description: A Commvault API access token is required. Follow the [Commvault documentation](https://documentation.commvault.com/2024e/essential/creating_access_token.html) to create an access token with appropriate permissions.

customs.name: Commvault Environment Endpoint URL
customs.description: The Commvault Cloud API endpoint URL is required (format: https://hostname/commandcenter/api).

instructionSteps.title: 1. Obtain Commvault API Credentials
instructionSteps.description:
Before configuring the connector, you need to obtain API credentials from your Commvault environment.

1. Follow the [Commvault documentation](https://documentation.commvault.com/2024e/essential/creating_access_token.html) to create an API access token
2. Ensure the user creating the token has **Admin** or **Tenant Admin** privileges
3. Note your Commvault Cloud API endpoint URL (format: https://hostname/commandcenter/api)

instructionSteps.title: 2. Configure the Commvault Security IQ Connector
instructionSteps.description: Provide your Commvault API credentials and endpoint information to establish the connection.

instructionSteps.title: 3. Verify Connection
instructionSteps.description:
After connecting, verify that data is flowing into Microsoft Sentinel:

1. Navigate to **Logs** in Microsoft Sentinel
2. Run the query: `CommvaultSecurityIQ_CL | take 10`
3. Data should appear within 5-10 minutes of configuration

**Note**: The connector collects security events based on configured event levels (Major, Critical, and Anomalous events by default).
